factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with some growth in certain segments but sluggish overall.

evidence.text: Excluding these effects, results reflected contribution from improved customer contract signings over the past twelve months, which was partially offset by sluggish underlying business activity.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, particularly from large customers and managed spend accounts.

evidence.text: This was helped by the absence of Good Friday in March of 2025. Excluding these effects, results reflected contribution from improved customer contract signings over the past twelve months, which was partially offset by sluggish underlying business activity.

factors.summary: The company is not experiencing significant pricing power as price levels remained relatively stable.

evidence.text: The impact of product pricing on net sales was not material in the first quarter of 2025 and 2024. Price levels remained relatively stable in the first quarter of 2025.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, impacting gross profit.

evidence.text: Our gross profit percentage was primarily impacted by three factors. First, customer and product mix diluted our gross profit percentage. This reflects relatively stronger growth from large customers, including Onsite-like customers, and non-fastener products, each of which tend to have a lower gross profit percentage than our business as a whole.

factors.summary: Organic growth is slowing with net sales growth at 3.4% year-over-year, down from previous periods.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $64.3, or 3.4%, in the first quarter of 2025 when compared to the first quarter of 2024. There was one less selling day in the first quarter of 2025 relative to the prior year period and, taking this into consideration, our net daily sales increased 5.0% in the first quarter of 2025 compared to the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased customer sites and average sales per site.

evidence.text: We experienced an increase in unit sales in the first quarter of 2025. This was due to a growth in the number of customer sites spending $10K or more per month with Fastenal and, to a lesser degree, growth in average monthly sales per customer site across all customer spend categories.

factors.summary: Manufacturing end markets are outperforming, while non-residential construction is underperforming.

evidence.text: Our manufacturing end markets outperformed primarily due to the relative strength we are experiencing with key account customers with significant managed spend where our service model and technology are particularly impactful. This disproportionately benefits manufacturing customers. Other end market sales were favorably impacted by growth with warehousing and storage, and data center customers, which was partially offset by declining sales with resellers.

factors.summary: Input costs are increasing, particularly in fleet and transportation costs, impacting gross profit.

evidence.text: Second, we experienced higher fleet and transportation costs due to inflation in vehicle costs as we cycle our fleet and in third-party freight costs.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher average FTE and wages, partially offset by lower incentive pay.

evidence.text: We experienced a modest increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE and, to a lesser degree, higher average wages during the period, as well as higher health insurance costs. This was partly offset by lower incentive pay and profit sharing expense versus the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Margins are compressing, particularly in gross profit, due to mix and cost pressures.

evidence.text: Our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, decreased to 45.1% in the first quarter of 2025 from 45.5% in the first quarter of 2024. Our gross profit percentage was primarily impacted by three factors.

factors.summary: Cost actions are being implemented, but their impact is limited by selling day changes and cost increases.

evidence.text: Our ability to leverage was impacted by having one less selling day in the first quarter of 2025 as compared to the first quarter of 2024, as operating expenses are not managed around monthly selling days. We also experienced growth in SG&A of 3.9% in the first quarter of 2025 versus net sales growth of 3.4% in the same period.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident in FMI technology adoption, though challenges remain.

evidence.text: The table below summarizes signings and installations of our FMI devices and sales through our FMI devices, eBusiness (1) tools, and Digital Footprint (2).

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with ongoing investments in distribution and transportation.

evidence.text: We had an increase in our distribution and transportation FTE personnel of 98 to support increased product throughput at our distribution facilities.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing in manufacturing and distribution, signaling confidence.

evidence.text: We had an increase in our distribution and transportation FTE personnel of 98 to support increased product throughput at our distribution facilities.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed effectively, with no significant risks noted.

evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: No significant quality or safety incidents reported.

evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Management has not provided explicit forward guidance in the text.

evidence.text: Management has not provided explicit forward guidance in the text.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2 — MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Management appears cautious with investments in workforce and technology.

evidence.text: We had an increase in our distribution and transportation FTE personnel of 98 to support increased product throughput at our distribution facilities. We had an increase in our remaining FTE personnel of 134 which related primarily to personnel investments in manufacturing, quality control, IT, and business analytics.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include sluggish business conditions and foreign exchange impacts.

evidence.text: Excluding these effects, results reflected contribution from improved customer contract signings over the past twelve months, which was partially offset by sluggish underlying business activity. Changes in foreign exchange rates negatively affected sales in the first quarter of 2025 by approximately 50 basis points as compared to having an immaterial impact in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy focuses on customer service, technology, and market expansion, with investments in FMI and digital solutions.

evidence.text: We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain. We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange rates and economic conditions affecting demand.

evidence.text: Changes in foreign exchange rates negatively affected sales in the first quarter of 2025 by approximately 50 basis points as compared to having an immaterial impact in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Catalysts include customer signings and increased FMI device installations.

evidence.text: We signed 6,418 weighted FASTBin and FASTVend devices in the first quarter of 2025. Our goal for weighted FASTBin and FASTVend device signings in 2025 remains between 28,000 to 30,000 MEUs.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with a focus on gaining share through customer signings and technology.

evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable with a focus on differentiation through customer service and technology.

evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through technology and customer service.

evidence.text: We believe this close-to-the-customer, 'high-touch, high-tech' partnership approach is differentiated in the marketplace and allows us to gain market share in what remains a fragmented industrial distribution market.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a risk with reliance on key accounts.

evidence.text: Sales with our contract customers continue to outperform as we realize incremental sales from implementing strong customer signings that we have achieved over the last 12 months, which was partially offset by subdued business activity. Non-contract customers tend to be smaller and utilize fewer of our tools and capabilities, providing fewer avenues for share gains and therefore more closely reflect business trends, which remain sluggish.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind with sluggish manufacturing and overall business activity.

evidence.text: Excluding these effects, results reflected contribution from improved customer contract signings over the past twelve months, which was partially offset by sluggish underlying business activity.

factors.summary: Interest rates are impacting net income with lower interest income.

evidence.text: We had lower interest income reflecting a reduction in capital being invested in higher-earning short-term instruments during the period. We had lower interest expense as a result of slightly lower borrowings through the first quarter of 2025. The greater reduction in interest income relative to interest expense resulted in our generating net interest expense of $0.8 in the first quarter of 2025, compared to $0.4 in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: FX exposure is a headwind with negative impacts on sales.

evidence.text: Changes in foreign exchange rates negatively affected sales in the first quarter of 2025 by approximately 50 basis points as compared to having an immaterial impact in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are not explicitly mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: Geopolitical risks are not explicitly mentioned in the text.

While there is no change to total absolute or total FTE headcount, it produces minor shifts between headcount categories. Historical numbers have been adjusted to reflect this realignment.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are mixed with a positive trend in dividends and stable net income.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2025, we returned $246.7 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to $223.2 in the first quarter of 2024.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: Our diluted net income per share was $0.52 in the first quarter of 2025, compared to $0.52 in the first quarter of 2024.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures, signaling confidence in future growth, particularly in distribution and transportation to support increased product throughput.

evidence.text: For 2025, we continue to expect our net capital expenditures to be within a range of $265.0 to $285.0, an increase from $214.1 in 2024.

factors.summary: The company is showing a trend of deleveraging, as evidenced by a decrease in total debt as a percentage of capital from 5.5% to 5.1%. However, net cash used in financing activities has decreased by 11.9%, which could indicate either improved liquidity or reduced funding needs.

evidence.text: Total debt on our balance sheet was $200.0 at the end of the first quarter of 2025, or 5.1% of total capital, compared to 5.5% at the end of the first quarter of 2024.
